title: Command Parser — Text Processing
description: Create a CommandParser class that parses player text input into structured commands. Use string methods or regex to handle: 'go north', 'take sword', 'attack goblin', 'look', 'inventory', 'say hello everyone', 'use potion', and 'help'. The parse() method returns a dict with 'command' and 'args' keys.
test_code:
parser = CommandParser()
r = parser.parse('go north')
assert r['command'] == 'go', f"Expected 'go', got '{r['command']}'"
assert r['args'] == 'north', f"Expected 'north', got '{r['args']}'"
r = parser.parse('take iron sword')
assert r['command'] == 'take', f"Expected 'take', got '{r['command']}'"
assert r['args'] == 'iron sword', f"Expected 'iron sword', got '{r['args']}'"
r = parser.parse('attack goblin')
assert r['command'] == 'attack', f"Expected 'attack'"
assert r['args'] == 'goblin', f"Expected 'goblin'"
r = parser.parse('look')
assert r['command'] == 'look', f"Expected 'look'"
assert r['args'] == '', f"Expected empty args for look, got '{r['args']}'"
r = parser.parse('inventory')
assert r['command'] == 'inventory', f"Expected 'inventory'"
assert r['args'] == '', f"Expected empty args for inventory"
r = parser.parse('say hello everyone')
assert r['command'] == 'say', f"Expected 'say'"
assert r['args'] == 'hello everyone', f"Expected 'hello everyone', got '{r['args']}'"
r = parser.parse('use potion')
assert r['command'] == 'use', f"Expected 'use'"
assert r['args'] == 'potion', f"Expected 'potion'"
r = parser.parse('help')
assert r['command'] == 'help', f"Expected 'help'"
r = parser.parse('dance wildly')
assert r['command'] == 'unknown', f"Expected 'unknown' for unrecognized command"
assert 'dance wildly' in r['args'], 'unknown command should preserve original input in args'
acceptance_criteria: CommandParser.parse() returns a dict with 'command' and 'args' keys
Handles all 8 command types: go, take, attack, look, inventory, say, use, help
Multi-word arguments are preserved (e.g., 'say hello everyone')
Single-word commands like 'look' and 'help' have empty string args
Unrecognized commands return command='unknown' with original input in args
hints: Strip the input and split on the first space: parts = text.strip().split(None, 1)
First part is the command word, second part (if any) is the args
Check if the command word is in KNOWN_COMMANDS
If no second part exists (single word), args should be empty string
